title: Xtra Fluffy Kenyan Egg Spring Rolls
description: A funky and fluffy Kenyan dish featuring egg roll wrappers, leaf wrap steam, and ovo-vegetarian friendly ingredients, cooked to perfection in a tandoor clay pot.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor clay pot to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the beaten eggs, chopped onion, grated carrots, and shredded cabbage.
Season the egg mixture with salt and pepper to taste.
Lay an egg roll wrapper on a flat surface and place about 1/4 cup of the egg mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Brush the edges of the wrapper with a small amount of water.
Fold the bottom half of the wrapper over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll up the wrapper to form a tight cylinder.
Repeat the process with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Place the spring rolls in the preheated tandoor clay pot, leaving about 1 inch of space between each roll.
Cover the pot with a leaf wrapper and steam the spring rolls for 10 minutes.
Remove the spring rolls from the pot and fry them in hot oil until golden brown and crispy.
